C[ericosman: I know not enough about opening theory to know whether ""high"" at E4 or ""low"" at E3 or invade at R17 is better, or if it is just a matter of taste.  When It's just a matter of taste, I don't want to give it effort, but if one is better than another, it's nice to know why
C[Nathan: ah yes I am not an expert on fuseki
Nathan: and it feels like things have changed with AI and I am not up to date on the latest
C[ericosman: I have to assume that the areas in which AI has changed things in the opening is only a difference of a point or less
Nathan: yes that is why I dont usually worry, at our level games are usually decided by bigger mistakes than that
ericosman: so I guess my question about those three spots on the board was more wondering if one of them was more than one point better than the two others
C[ericosman: similar question here, black invade upper right, or attach as I did, or something else instead?  Again, if less than one point difference I'm not really interested
C[ericosman: nathan I see that basicpract is watching who is actually John Kerpan, a good go teacher.  often he is available after the game to review it if you have time.
C[ericosman: black wasn't sure whether to push at p6 or at o5

C[ericosman: By the way, Nathan, if you want to make tactical or strategic comments or questions without me seeing them until after the game, you can click right on the word ""chat"" and it will turn green and change to ""malkovich"".
Nathan: I am seeing your comments so far
C[ericosman: yes, I am using ""chat"" and not ""malkovich""
C[ericosman: we're certainly playing in a passionate direction!
ericosman: I don't know whether john has a go lecture tonight.  he often does a twitch lecture which is fun, but if he has one, he won't be available to review our game
Nathan: okay i think the league does reviews tomorrow right?
